snippet: The principal or nominated person must be satisfied that the student has the capacity to undertake a structured workplace learning placement without exposing themselves or others in the workplace to any unreasonable level of risk. The protection of the safety, health, development and wellbeing of students undertaking structured workplace learning is of paramount importance to the department. This section lists a number of industries, activities, plant, equipment and workplace hazards which present unreasonable risks to students’ health and safety and/or which require extensive training or experience to perform safely and are therefore prohibited or restricted for structured workplace learning students. The lists are not exhaustive but are intended to support principals or nominated persons to fulfil their duty of care responsibilities when entering into structured workplace learning arrangements. School staff, employers and workplace supervisors need to be aware that structured workplace learning students: To create and maintain a child safe organisation, all Victorian schools must comply with Ministerial Order 1359 – Implementing the Child Safe Standards – Managing the risk of child abuse in schools (PDF) External Link . The Child Safe Standards require schools to develop and implement risk management strategies to ensure students’ safety in the school environment. The school environment includes workplace learning environments where students undertake work experience, structured workplace learning, school community work (volunteering) and school-based apprenticeships and traineeships.
